name: Kenya
intro: Kenya anchors East Africa's project economy: Nairobi hosts the regional headquarters of global firms, the UN's African hub, a banking sector exporting fintech across the continent, and infrastructure programmes from expressways to geothermal plants. Development-sector employers — NGOs, agencies, multilaterals — are among the region's biggest buyers of certified project management, and they specify credentials explicitly in hiring. PMP and Scrum certifications both travel well here. Our live online weekend batch runs on the IST schedule, converting to comfortable mid-morning to evening Kenyan time. You train in English with an international cohort, with invoices suited to employer or donor-funded sponsorship. Afterwards you get exam application guidance, full-length mock exams, and direct trainer access for questions until you clear the exam.

faq.q: How much does certification training cost in Kenya?
faq.a: The course fee is US$1,295 for PMP and US$695 for CSM, covering the full live online weekend workshop, study materials, mock exams and exam application support. PMI's PMP exam fee (US$425 member / US$675 non-member) is paid separately to PMI; the CSM exam is included in the course fee.

faq.q: Are the classes live or recorded?
faq.a: Every batch is taught live by an instructor over two weekend days — not pre-recorded videos. You can ask questions in real time, work through exercises with the cohort, and reach the trainer afterwards for doubts.

faq.q: Is the certification recognised by employers in Kenya?
faq.a: Yes. PMP (from PMI) and CSM (from Scrum Alliance) are global credentials recognised identically everywhere, including by employers and recruiters across Kenya.
